claim: Terminal-based interaction is better for AI agents than GUI-based computer use because text is the native modality for language models and terminal tasks are more directly representable and verifiable than GUI actions
actionable: Design agent benchmarks around terminal/CLI interaction rather than GUI clicking when evaluating model capabilities in constrained settings
source_title: Terminal-Bench: Pushing Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Factory Droid, et al to the limits

claim: Separating model capability from agent framework optimization requires a minimal agent (like Terminus) that only exposes basic tools (headless terminal) without special prompt engineering or tool design
actionable: Build evaluation frameworks with two tracks: one measuring raw model capability through Terminus-like minimal agents, and another measuring optimized agent performance with custom tools and prompts
source_title: Terminal-Bench: Pushing Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Factory Droid, et al to the limits

claim: The biggest performance lever for agents is the underlying model quality, not agent framework design; model selection matters ~2-3x more than agent optimization
actionable: Prioritize model improvements and selection over engineering elaborate agent frameworks when building production agentic systems
source_title: Terminal-Bench: Pushing Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Factory Droid, et al to the limits

claim: Future agent evaluation should be multi-dimensional, measuring not just accuracy but also cost-per-task and economic value generated rather than single-axis accuracy metrics
actionable: Design evaluation dashboards with 2D+ metrics showing accuracy vs. cost, accuracy vs. latency, and ideally revenue generated per dollar spent on inference
source_title: Terminal-Bench: Pushing Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Factory Droid, et al to the limits

claim: Open-source benchmarks are becoming more valuable as easy tasks get saturated; future benchmarks will focus on long-horizon tasks with multiple files, unique environments, and real-world workflows rather than scraped public datasets
actionable: When creating benchmarks, prioritize tasks that cannot be scraped from the internet, require multi-step exploration of complex codebases, and come from real user workflows
source_title: Terminal-Bench: Pushing Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Factory Droid, et al to the limits
